headline: Third-ranked Arkansas seeks statement win at struggling Clemson
subhead: The Razorbacks enter Tiger territory as heavy favorites, but a defensive collapse by the home team offers opportunity.

xFactor.why: Clemson's defense allowed 41 points to LSU and must tighten dramatically or Arkansas will exploit soft coverage downfield.

keyMatchup: Arkansas's balanced offensive approach will test whether Clemson's staff can scheme meaningful coverage adjustments after surrendering 448 yards last week.
body: Arkansas rolls in as the clear favorite, riding a comfortable win over UCLA and holding the third-best ranking in the PCFL. The Razorbacks' offense has shown it can control pace and finish drives, which should pose real problems for a Clemson defense that imploded against LSU. The home team's secondary looked thoroughly overmatched in that 41-10 loss, and there's little time for wholesale corrections before Friday kickoff.

Clemson's path to staying competitive hinges entirely on whether the coaching room can restore basic execution in pass coverage and reduce the explosive plays that burned them so badly last week. Arkansas won't need to be perfect to win this one, but the Razorbacks will need to avoid self-inflicted wounds and let their front seven pressure early. A third-ranked program visiting a struggling ACC opponent should win convincingly, and the recent tape suggests nothing has changed to suggest otherwise.
